completed_tasks.name: Vercel build fix — prisma generate in build script, exclude prisma/ from TS
UX: always start wizard at step 0 on hydration (localStorage step restore bug)
Bug: useMigration deleteV1State() missing in validation-error path

IncidentList alerts: generic yellow/red → SIAG palette (#D44E17 orange, #CC0033 red)
Step1Einstieg: aria-pressed on toggle buttons + role=group
UAT session with SIAG consultant + collect written sign-off → tag v1.1.0

human_actions_pending.action: Smoke test production app — verify header visible in both light and dark mode
human_actions_pending.context: Header background was transparent (bg-siag-navy not a Tailwind utility); fixed with inline style. Needs visual verification.

human_actions_pending.action: Schedule UAT session with SIAG consultant using docs/uat/UAT_CHECKLISTE.md
human_actions_pending.context: All code complete, UAT docs translated to German. Sign-off required for v1.1.0 tag.

decisions.decision: Use style={{ backgroundColor: '#003B5E' }} in Header instead of @theme for siag-navy
decisions.rationale: Tailwind v4 @theme conflicts with :root CSS vars of same name. Inline style is guaranteed.

decisions.decision: WizardContext HYDRATE always resets currentStep to 0
decisions.rationale: Restoring mid-wizard step confused users; form data still restored, navigation resets to beginning.

decisions.decision: Prisma 7: datasource url moved to prisma.config.ts, DIRECT_URL used for migrations
decisions.rationale: Prisma 7 breaking change — url/directUrl no longer allowed in schema.prisma.

next_action: Visually verify production at https://siag-incident-assistant.vercel.app — check header visible in light mode, logo correct, buttons red (SIAG-Rot). Then address dark mode text-navy visibility issue.
context_notes: Session focused on: (1) unblocking Vercel deployment after Prisma 7 upgrade, (2) fixing step-5-on-load UX bug, (3) SIAG CI/UX audit. The @theme Tailwind v4 issue was the trickiest — CSS vars in :root do NOT auto-generate Tailwind utilities; only @theme vars do. But @theme conflicts with same-named :root vars. Solution: inline style for the header.
